rationale: The cited paper is used as a methodological source for how to probe and interpret interaction effects via Johnson-Neyman floodlight analysis. In multiple studies, the authors explicitly report conducting JN floodlight analyses and cite Spiller et al. (2013) when identifying regions of significance for their moderation results. This is not a passing background mention; it directly supports the paper's core hypothesis-testing procedure for moderated effects.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Spiller et al. (2013)'s spotlight/floodlight guidance, especially Johnson-Neyman probing, to analyze and interpret interaction effects between relationship power and partner behavior. The cited work functions as an analysis framework for conditional-effect interpretation rather than as a target of theoretical extension or critique.
